title: Google Chat Integration Guide
content:
## Overview

Google Chat is a communication and collaboration platform that is part of Google Workspace. It provides direct messaging, group conversations (spaces), threaded discussions, and deep integration with other Google Workspace tools like Google Drive, Google Meet, and Google Calendar. The T1 Agentics integration with Google Chat enables SOC teams to send security alerts and notifications to Google Chat spaces, coordinate incident response, and communicate with stakeholders through Google Workspace.

**Vendor:** Google
**Category:** Communication & Collaboration
**Authentication:** Service Account (OAuth 2.0) or Incoming Webhook

## Prerequisites

Before configuring the Google Chat integration in T1 Agentics, ensure you have:

- A Google Workspace account with Google Chat enabled
- For full API access (Google Chat API):
  - A Google Cloud project with the Google Chat API enabled
  - A service account created in the Google Cloud Console:
    - Navigate to **IAM & Admin > Service Accounts > Create Service Account**
    - Generate a JSON key file for the service account
  - Domain-wide delegation configured (if sending messages on behalf of users)
  - The Chat app published in Google Workspace Marketplace (or configured for internal use)
  - Required scopes:
    - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.messages.create`
    - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chat.spaces.readonly`
- For Webhook integration (simpler, send-only):
  - An incoming webhook configured in a Google Chat space:
    - Open the target space > **Apps & integrations > Manage webhooks > Add webhook**
    - Copy the webhook URL
- Network access from T1 Agentics to the Google Chat API (`https://chat.googleapis.com`)

## Configuration in T1 Agentics

1. Navigate to **Connect > Marketplace** in the T1 Agentics sidebar
2. Search for **Google Chat** or browse the **Communication** category
3. Click on the Google Chat card and select **Configure**
4. Enter your connection details:
   - **Connection Name**: A descriptive label (e.g., "Google Chat - SOC Space")
   - **Integration Method**: Select Service Account or Webhook
   - For Service Account:
     - **Service Account Key**: Upload or paste the JSON key file contents
     - **Default Space**: The default Google Chat space for notifications
   - For Webhook:
     - **Webhook URL**: The Google Chat incoming webhook URL
5. Click **Test Connection** to verify access
6. Click **Save** to activate the integration
7. The integration will appear in your **Connect > Active Integrations** list

## Available Actions

| Action | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| Send Message | Generic | Post a text message to a Google Chat space |
| Send Webhook Message | Generic | Send a message via incoming webhook |
| Send Card Message | Generic | Send a rich card message with sections, widgets, and buttons |
| Create Space | Generic | Create a new Google Chat space for incident response |
| List Spaces | Investigate | List all spaces the app has access to |
| Get Space | Investigate | Retrieve details of a specific space |
| List Members | Investigate | List members of a specific space |
| Add Member | Generic | Add a user to a Google Chat space |
| Send Thread Reply | Generic | Reply to a specific thread in a space |
| Update Message | Generic | Edit a previously sent message |
| Delete Message | Generic | Delete a message from a space |

## Common Use Cases

- **Security alert notifications**: Send formatted security alert cards to dedicated Google Chat spaces with severity indicators, alert details, and action buttons
- **Incident response spaces**: Create dedicated Google Chat spaces for major security incidents, automatically adding relevant responders from the SOC team
- **Google Workspace security events**: Correlate Google Workspace security events with Google Chat notifications for organizations fully invested in the Google ecosystem
- **Threaded discussions**: Use thread replies to keep investigation updates organized within a single thread per incident, maintaining conversation clarity
- **Webhook-based quick alerting**: Set up simple webhook-based alerting for rapid deployment without the overhead of a full Chat API application

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| Service account authentication fails | Verify the JSON key file is complete and not corrupted. Ensure the Google Chat API is enabled in the Google Cloud project |
| 403 Permission denied | The service account lacks required permissions, or domain-wide delegation is not configured. Check the Google Workspace Admin Console |
| Webhook returns 400 | The webhook message payload must follow Google Chat's card/message format. Validate the JSON structure |
| Space not found | Verify the space name/ID is correct. The Chat app must be added to the space before it can send messages |
| Messages not appearing in space | Ensure the Chat app is installed in the target space. For service accounts, the app must be added to each space it needs to post in |
| Rate limiting | Google Chat API has quota limits. Check your Google Cloud Console for current quota usage and request increases if needed |
